title: Apps can require deps directly (requiredDeps) — untax non-app users
description:
SYSTEM CHANGE. Blocks MPI-299 (Head Swap) and every future app with app-only weights.

PROBLEM: AppDef.requiredModels resolves MODEL ids only (appsRegistry.js appAvailability() filters state.s_installedModelIds). An app has NO way to require a dep directly. Today the only route for an app-specific extra weight is to add it to a MODEL's dependency list — which taxes EVERY user of that model.

CONCRETE CASE: Head Swap needs qwen-edit PLUS a 1.2GB head-swap LoRA (bfs_head_v5_2511_merged_version_rank_32_fp32.safetensors; an fp16 variant exists and is being A/B tested — may be ~half). Folding it into qwen-edit forces 1.2GB on every Qwen user for one dev-gated app. User's scaling argument: an app taking 30 style LoRAs would tax all users ~15GB. Unacceptable.

PROPOSED SHAPE (agreed direction, confirm at implementation):
  {
    id: 'head-swap',
    requiredModels: ['qwen-edit'],          // shared model
    requiredDeps:   ['qwen-lora-headswap'], // app-only extras
  }
requiredDeps = dep ids resolved from DEPS (js/data/modelConstants/dependencies.js facade). Works for LoRAs, support weights AND custom nodes — nodesDeps is already in DEPS, so an app needing e.g. comfyui-inpaint-cropandstitch just lists it.

WHY NOT a new appDeps.js split file (user's initial idea, discussed 2026-07-18): the four dep files are split by KIND (modelDeps/assetDeps/loraDeps/nodesDeps), not by owner. A head-swap LoRA IS a LoRA — filing it by owner would put same-kind entries in two homes and force footprint.js/GC/installer to know both. Ownership belongs in a LIST OF IDS (requiredDeps), not a file. Monolith pressure (models.js 56KB, loraDeps 15KB) is real but the right lever is splitting FURTHER BY KIND when a file gets fat (loraDeps.js -> loras/krea2.js, loras/qwen.js); the facade absorbs that with zero consumer changes. Keeps one rule: entries filed by WHAT THEY ARE.

SCOPE / ROOT-CAUSE WARNING: this touches dep resolution = a SHARED PRIMITIVE. Per CLAUDE.md ROOT-CAUSE RULE: grep + classify EVERY consumer, fix local AND remote (Pod) engine twins in ONE pass. A Head-Swap-shaped patch on one call site = false done. Surfaces to sweep: appsRegistry.js appAvailability(), resolveModelDeps.js, the App Library Install button flow (MpiAppLibrary slide-over, per-model download drive), installStore/reconciler, footprint.js (does an app's deps count toward a model's footprint? probably NOT), GC/cleanup (app deps must not be GC'd as orphans), remote/Pod install path. [[feedback_engine_split_sweep_all_consumers]] [[feedback_check_both_engine_paths]]

OPEN: whether requiredDeps entries show individually in the App Library slide-over's per-model install list, or aggregate under one 'extras' row.
